question: WHAT TO AVOID ON THE LANES?
content:
never try to do something you haven’t practiced for during competition if you want to succeed.
stay away from penalty zones.
try to use traffic on your beneffit instead of fighting against it.
stay focus and don’t loose oportunities to learn something new: when nothing is working, still can be a profitable day.
not keeping a grip perfect at anytime. use tapes, change them, keep inserts clean etc
not paying attention to others traffic, to know what zones to avoid and what areas seem to give some room.
When reading the lane, the biggest thing to avoid is playing in the wrong part of the lane for the traffic around you. Other bowlers change the pattern fast, and if you stand in the wrong zone, your reaction will disappear quickly.
Here’s what to avoid:
1. Avoid “penalty zones”
These are areas where the ball either hooks instantly or never hooks at all.
In these spots, you can’t miss even one board without paying for it.
2. Avoid staying on lines that other players are burning up
If many bowlers are playing the same part of the lane, the oil disappears fast.
That means that the ball hooks earlier, the breakpoint moves and your line becomes unpredictable
Stay alert to where the traffic is.
3. Avoid following high rev players if you can’t match their angles
High rev bowlers break down the lane quickly.
If you stand on top of them, the lane will change faster than you can adjust.
4. Avoid ignoring carrydown
If some bowlers are pushing oil downlane, your ball may skid too long, miss the breakpoint and leave weak corners
Don’t keep throwing into an area that has lost its backend.
5. Avoid stubbornly staying where you want to play
Your comfort zone doesn’t matter if the lane doesn’t allow it.
Let the ball motion guide you, not your preference.
